title: MOE Tuition Fee Loan (TFL) & Study Loan (SL) Schemes
text:
MOE Tuition Fee Loan (TFL) & Study Loan (SL) Schemes - SupportGoWhere
MOE Tuition Fee Loan (TFL) & Study Loan (SL) Schemes
Share link
From 1 July 2026, MOE will be implementing a new Higher Education Student Loan (HESL) scheme to replace the existing government loan schemes - Tuition Fee Loan (TFL), Study Loan (SL) and Overseas Student Programme Loan (OSPL), which will be merged under the new HESL scheme. Institutions will stop accepting new TFL, SL and OSPL applications before 1 June 2026.

If you intend to apply for loan before 1 July 2026, please check with your institution regarding the respective cut-off dates for TFL, SL and OSPL application. If you wish to apply for a loan after the cut-off dates, you can apply for the HESL via a new portal from 1 July 2026. For details on the HESL scheme and its application process, please visit MOE website (https://www.moe.gov.sg/financial-matters/government-loan-schemes) or your institution website.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
TFL and means-tested SL schemes can cover up to 100% of subsidised tuition fees for eligible Singapore Citizen students
Eligible students who meet the income eligibility criteria can also apply for a living allowance loan to cover their education expenses under the means-tested SL scheme
Loans are interest-free during the course of study; interest and repayment commences only upon a student’s graduation or leaving the institution
Collapse all sections
About this support

For TFL

For Polytechnics, LASALLE College of the Arts (LASALLE) or Nanyang Academy of Fine Arts (NAFA) diploma students

	

Loan coverage of up to 75% of subsidised tuition fees payable.




For Autonomous University students, or LASALLE or NAFA undergraduates

	

Loan coverage of up to 90% of subsidised tuition fees payable.




For SL

For Undergraduates

	

Covers up to 10% of the subsidised Singapore Citizen tuition fees payable and a living allowance loan of $3,600 per year




For Diploma Students

	

Covers up to 25% of subsidised tuition fees payable and a living allowance loan of $2,000 per year

Who is this for?

For TFL

Students studying full-time MOE-subsidised diploma courses in any of the local polytechnics.

Students studying full-time MOE-subsidised undergraduate and postgraduate programmes in autonomous universities.

Singapore Citizen (SC) and Singapore Permanent Resident (PR) students studying full-time MOE-subsidised diploma and undergraduate programmes in LASALLE or NAFA.

SC students studying part-time MOE-subsidised undergraduate programmes in  autonomous universities.




For SL

For Undergraduates

SC, PR or international students taking full-time MOE-subsidised undergraduate courses in autonomous universities.

SC taking part-time MOE-subsidised undergraduate courses in autonomous universities. 

SC and PR taking full-time MOE-subsidised undergraduate courses in LASALLE or NAFA.

For Diploma Students

SC or PR taking full-time MOE-subsidised diploma courses at Polytechnics, LASALLE and NAFA.

How to apply?

For TFL

Students in Nanyang Technological University or National University of Singapore can approach DBS or OCBC.

Students in Singapore Management University can approach OCBC.

All other students can approach DBS.




For SL

Students can approach the respective post-secondary education institutions for application.

Where can I find help?

View more details

Submit an enquiry

Singapore 138675

Scheme last updated 19 May 2026
